title: In /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public, inspect local implementation scripts/local/integrations/note-draft-upload.js and compare with any references under /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/note-generate. Focus only on likely causes of blank note drafts after POST /api/v1/text...
summary_text: Likely causes (focused on payload/endpoint contract), comparing local uploader vs the `note-generate` reference: 1. **Blank/near-empty `body` payload in `draft_save`** - In local (`scripts/local/integrations/note-draft-upload.js`), `body` is passed as `markdownToNoteBody(stripLeadingTitleHeading(...))`. - `stripLeadingTitleHeading` removes `# ${title}` when matched exactly. If the input content is only the title line plus unsupported/empty content (or parser strips most content), `body` can be empty or minimal while API still returns success. - Field of interest: `body` in `POST /api/v1/text_notes/draft_save?id=...`. 2. **No semantic validation of `draft_save` response** - Both scripts only await a successful HTTP response; they do not assert that persisted content changed. - If `POST /api/v1/text_notes/draft_save` now accepts envelope-level success while ignoring content, the script still reports success. - Field/contract risk: any success response shape that does not imply content persistence for `name`, `body`, `index` is a false-positive. 3. **Identifier ambiguity (`noteId` vs `noteKey`) and double-write behavior** - Local does an extra second `draft_save` when `noteKey` differs from `noteId`: - first `id=<noteId>` - then `id=<noteKey>` - If `created.data.id` and `created.data.key` are not the same semantic id (or one is stale/alias-like), you can update a different resource than the one you open via `noteUrl`. - Fields: `noteId`, `noteKey`, query param `id`. 4. **Endpoint contract drift risk around payload shape** - Both scripts send `{ name, body, index }`, but if note.com changed expected fields (e.g., `title` vs `name`, or requires additional flags for content type), API may return success while ignoring/normalizing to empty content. - Fields: `name`, `body`, `index` in step-2 payload. 5. **Auth/header contract sensitivity** - Both scripts still use cookie-only auth (`_note_session_v5`, optional `XSRF-TOKEN`) and no explicit XSRF header. - If note.com tightened write requirements, draft save may be acknowledged but content write path may be rejected/sanitized internally. - Fields/headers: `Cookie: _note_session_v5=...` (and optional `Cookie: XSRF-TOKEN=...`), plus endpoint `/api/v1/text_notes/draft_save`. 6. **Version/flow mismatch signal** - `SKILL.md` documents v3.2, while the reference script is v3.3 and local behavior diverges (e.g., title-stripping, extra key-based retry save). - Even if not a bug itself, this indicates possible drift from intended contract assumptions. - Contracts impacted: retry/field expectations across `POST /api/v1/text_notes` → `POST /api/v1/text_notes/draft_save`. High-confidence starting points to verify immediately in the outgoing request: - log/inspect `body.length` and the raw `draft_save` JSON payload just before Step 2, - confirm final `id` value used in URL (`noteId` vs `noteKey`), - compare response body from `draft_save` against an expected “content persisted” indicator (not just HTTP 2xx).
